text:
Once upon a time there was a small village surrounded by trees. There was nothing particularly extraordinary about the village; it simply thrived on tranquility and its own peculiar charm. In the heart of this peaceful place lived a wise old owl named Wilby.

Wilby lived alone apart from his beloved gnome mice. He had a beautiful view of the forest from his perch in the big oak tree at the village’s edge. Every morning he would scan his world with keen eyes before starting his day alone. His nights were even quieter, filled with gentle rustles as the forest slept.

One afternoon, however, something unexpected happened. A flock of beautiful goldfinches, accustomed to quieter environments, fluttered into Wilby’s tree looking for solitude. They weren’t used to such big eyes, especially one so wise as Wilby’s. For days the birds chirped awkwardly at his favorite perch, looking for somewhere more secluded.

Wilby wasn’t bothered. He simply enjoyed their company. Each
